hypometrics 0.0.9

    Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) Data

    cgm_data.Rmd

    1. Introduction

    The hypometrics package was developed to manipulate data from CGM devices manufactured by Abbott, specifically the Abbott Freestyle Libre 2 sensor and reader. Future work will involve updating the package so it can function with data from additional CGM devices (e.g. Dexcom).

    This article describes the CGM-specific functions that were created as part of the hypometrics package.

    Setup

    To be able to use the CGM functions, firstly install and load hypometrics.

    #Install
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("hypometrics")
    #Load package
library(hypometrics)

    Example data

    Throughout this tutorial, the examples presented will be based on the cgm dataframe.

    • This dataframe contains data from 3 participants who used a CGM device, with one row per CGM timestamp as shown below.
    utils::head(cgm)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>   id    cgm_timestamp       glucose
#>   <chr> <dttm>                <dbl>
#> 1 001   2020-10-30 14:12:00    4.1 
#> 2 001   2020-10-30 14:13:10    3.3 
#> 3 001   2020-10-30 14:14:10    3.38
#> 4 001   2020-10-30 14:15:10    3.47
#> 5 001   2020-10-30 14:16:10    3.55
#> 6 001   2020-10-30 14:17:10    3.63

    2. Cleaning CGM data

    Interpolation

    3. Analysing CGM data

    Hypoglycaemia episode detection

    The sdhDetection() function allows to automatically detect and report on every episode of CGM-detected hypoglycaemia. The function takes CGM data and returns a list of all episodes of hypoglycaemia including their characteristics such as duration and nadir. The default hypoglycaemia detection limit is 3.9 mmol/L and duration 15 minutes.

    4. Checking

    5. Visualising
